title: andropogon torreyanus
intro: Andropogon torreyanus, also known as broom grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Andropogon torreyanus is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and may also be used in poultices or salves for topical application.
